word_list.pronunciation: mˌeɪk ɐn ˈæs ˈaʊt ʌv wʌnsˈɛlf

senses.definition: to behave in a manner that is considered foolish or immature

examples.x: I know Greg is only trying to impress Lisa, but he's making an ass out of himself by telling so many corny jokes.
At the party, he made an ass of himself by dancing on the tables and spilling drinks everywhere.
She realized she had made an ass of herself by mispronouncing important names during her speech.
His attempts to impress his new colleagues ended up making an ass of himself when he tripped and fell.
Sometimes, it's better to stay quiet than risk making an ass out of yourself by saying the wrong thing.

senses.definition: said to mean something is done without caring if someone gets offended or upset

word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt lˈuːz ˌɛni slˈiːp ˌoʊvɚɹ ɐbˌaʊt ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to not be upset or concerned about someone or something at all

examples.x: I'm disappointed about their decision, but I'm not losing any sleep over it.
I won't lose sleep over the minor issues at work; they can be resolved in due time.
He doesn't lose sleep over the stock market fluctuations; he has a long-term investment strategy.
Moving to London is certainly a possibility - I wouldn't dismiss it out of hand.
When the fire alarm went off, the employees evacuated the building out of hand.
She agreed to help out of hand, demonstrating her willingness to assist immediately.
He paid the bill out of hand, not waiting for anyone else to cover their share.
In emergencies, people often respond out of hand, acting quickly to address the situation.

word_list.pronunciation: ɹˈʌn ɹˈʌfʃɑːd ˌoʊvɚ ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to not care about other people's opinions, feelings, or rights

examples.x: He achieved success by ruthlessly running roughshod over anyone who got in his way.
The new manager ran roughshod over the employees, making decisions without consulting them.
The company's expansion plans ran roughshod over the concerns of the local community.
He doesn't want to be the kind of leader who runs roughshod over his team but prefers to collaborate with them.

senses.definition: to do or say something that is hurtful to someone and makes them undergo deep distress

examples.x: Her thoughtless remark always cuts him to the quick.
His harsh words cut her to the quick, and she couldn't hold back her tears.
The criticism from her family cut her to the quick, leaving her feeling deeply hurt.
The rejection letter cut him to the quick, as he had put his heart and soul into the application.

senses.definition: to show concern for or care about someone or something

examples.x: I don't give a hoot about celebrity gossip; I find it unimportant and uninteresting.
He gave a hoot about environmental conservation, and that's why he spent his weekends cleaning up local parks.
I don't give a hoot about which team wins the championship; I'm not a sports fan.

senses.definition: to care about a person or thing

examples.x: I haven't cared a hang for the show ever since they killed off my favorite character.
I don't care a hang about sports, so I didn't watch the big game.
She didn't give a hang for fancy parties; she preferred quiet evenings at home.
I never cared a hang for celebrities' personal lives; it's their work that matters to me.

senses.definition: to not care about other people and do what one believes is best for oneself

examples.x: In a fiercely competitive job market, you often have to look out for number one to secure the best opportunities.
He decided to look out for number one and negotiate a better salary for himself.
When it comes to personal finances, it's essential to look out for number one and save for your future.
In a crisis, it's human nature to look out for number one, ensuring personal safety before helping others.
